title: Waymo issues recall to deal with a flooding problem
summary: Waymo has issued a software recall that makes its robotaxis more cautious around flooded areas. A "final remedy" is in the works.

title: Instructure strikes deal with hackers who breached it twice
summary: The maker of the Canvas school software said it "reached an agreement" with the hackers, but provided no guarantees that the hackers would not release the data or keep their word.

title: Dessn raises $6M for its production focused design tool
summary: A new startup called Dessn has raised $6M to build AI-powered design tools that work directly with production codebases.

title: TikTok now wants to be the place you book the trip you just saw on TikTok
summary: TikTok is systematically converting its discovery engine into a transaction layer, which both deepens user retention and opens entirely new revenue streams for its new owners.

title: AI is turning connected cars into pothole-finding machines
summary: Fleet management company Samsara has developed an AI model to detect different kinds of potholes and gauge how fast they're deteriorating.

title: Spotify’s launches a Wrapped-style recap of your entire listening history
summary: Spotify's new feature will show you the number of unique songs that you listened to until now

title: AI voice startup Vapi hits $500M valuation after winning Amazon Ring over 40 rivals
summary: Vapi says its enterprise business has grown 10-fold since early 2025 as companies shift customer support and sales calls to AI agents.

title: Amazon launches 30-minute delivery across the U.S.
summary: Amazon says its new ultra-fast delivery option brings groceries, household essentials, and other items to customers’ doors in 30 minutes.

title: Thinking Machines wants to build an AI that actually listens while it talks
summary: Right now, every AI model you've ever used works the same way. You talk, it listens. It responds, you listen. Thinking Machines is trying to change that by building a model that processes your input and generates a response at the same time, so it's more like a phone call than a text chain.

title: Riding an AI rally, Robinhood preps second retail venture IPO
summary: Robinhood files confidentially for its second venture fund, this time targeting growth and early-stage startups.

title: GM just laid off hundreds of IT workers to hire those with stronger AI skills
summary: Some of the positions focus on AI-native development, data engineering and analytics, cloud-based engineering, and agent and model development as well as prompt engineering and new AI workflows.

title: Finally, texts between Android and iPhone users can be end-to-end encrypted
summary: Google had urged Apple for years to support RCS texting to make communication between each company's devices more seamless.

title: Daniel Ek-backed defense tech Helsing to raise $1.2B at $18B valuation
summary: Five-year old European military drone startup Helsing is close to raising another monster round.

title: Bravo is creating unscripted microdramas for the Peacock app
summary: As microdrama apps like ReelShort and DramaBox quietly rake in billions, Peacock announced on Monday that it is launching two unscripted Bravo microdramas, which will stream in the Peacock app.

title: Digg tries again, this time as an AI news aggregator
summary: In an email to beta testers, the company said the site's goal is to "track the most influential voices in a space" and to surface the news that's actually worth "paying attention to."

title: ‘Too early’ to talk IPO, Redwood Materials’ incoming CFO says
summary: The battery-recycling and energy-storage company has hired former Tesla finance chief Deepak Ahuja, reuniting him with former Tesla CTO JB Straubel.

title: NYT’s Wordle to become a TV game show
summary: This will be the first time that The Times has collaborated with a TV broadcaster for an entertainment-based program, representing yet another pivot in the media company's attempt to build a sustainable digital subscription business as print revenue continues to decline.

title: TikTok launches an ad-free subscription plan in the UK
summary: Users who sign up for the plan won’t see ads on TikTok, and their data won’t be used for advertising purposes.

title: There aren’t enough rockets for space data centers — Cowboy Space raised $275M to build them
summary: The apparently insatiable demand for AI compute has data center entrepreneurs looking to the stars. There's a key problem: There aren't enough rockets to put data centers in orbit around Earth, and they're too expensive.

title: Discord launches Nitro Rewards, giving subscribers access to the base tier of Xbox Game Pass for no extra cost
summary: Discord's Nitro Rewards program will give Nitro subscribers access to Xbox Game Pass and discounts from Logitech, SteelSeries, and other gaming brands.

title: Google’s Aluminium OS revealed in 16-minute leaked video
summary: Aluminium OS, Google's upcoming version of Android for PC, may have just leaked a few hours before Google's Android Show presentation. As Android Authority reports, leaker Mystic Leaks shared a 16-minute video on their Telegram channel that appears to show a lengthy hands-on demo of the new operating system. After a loading screen featuring the […]

title: There’s an internet choke point in the Middle East — is the solution in the North Pole?
summary: The vast majority of the world's data - emails, financial transactions, the internet - is carried by fiber optic cables that run along the ocean floor and converge at a few narrow choke points. Periodically, policymakers will release reports noting that this arrangement seems risky, but these routes are the shortest, often in use since […]

title: Amazon’s ultrafast 30-minute deliveries are now available in more cities
summary: Following a limited test run in parts of Seattle and Philadelphia that started last December, Amazon Now is officially available to millions of Amazon shoppers across the United States. In addition to Seattle and Philadelphia, the 30-minute delivery service is now also widely available in Atlanta and Dallas-Fort Worth, but will soon be expanding to […]

title: Waymo recalls robotaxis for driving on flooded roads
summary: Waymo is recalling its autonomous driving software that allowed its vehicles to drive on flooded roads. The recall affects 3,791 vehicles that operate using Waymo's fifth and sixth generation systems. In documents filed with the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration, the Alphabet-owned company said that an unoccupied Waymo robotaxi "encountered an untraversable flooded section of […]

title: Canvas owner reaches ‘agreement’ with hackers to secure stolen data
summary: Instructure, the company behind the Canvas learning management platform, says it has "reached an agreement" with hackers that breached its systems last week to prevent stolen data from being leaked online. The ShinyHunters hacking group claimed responsibility for the attack before Canvas was briefly taken offline. The group threatened to publish 3.5 terabytes of student […]

title: Spotify’s latest ‘Wrapped’ covers your entire music history
summary: Spotify is celebrating its 20th anniversary by giving listeners a "fully personalized look at their entire music history." The "Spotify 20: Your Party of the Year(s)" experience will allow users […]

title: How to watch Google’s Android Show: I/O Edition today
summary: Google I/O is still a week away, but Google's big announcements kick off today with the "Android Show: I/O Edition," where it's expected to announce the major Android ecosystem highlights coming to its annual developer conference. When the Android Show will happen and where you can watch it Android Show: I/O Edition will be streaming […]

title: eBay rejects GameStop’s $56 billion acquisition bid
summary: eBay has officially rejected GameStop's offer to buy the online marketplace for $56 billion, citing concerns around "operational risks" and how the acquisition will be financed. title: These new Roombas are smaller and cheaper
summary: iRobot has announced a new line of Roombas, a year after it debuted its first lidar-based robot vacuums. This week, it introduced eight robot vacuums that improve on its current lineup and feature lower prices, some up to £200 (about $270) cheaper. Key changes include higher suction power, a smaller overall size to better navigate […]

title: Sony upgraded its wearable AC so it’s cooler and better at hugging your neck
summary: Sony has announced a new version of the wearable personal air conditioner the company first revealed in 2019 ahead of the 2020 Tokyo Olympics. The latest version is an upgrade to last year's Pro model, and while the new Reon Pocket Pro Plus isn't a complete overhaul of the hardware, it comes with performance upgrades […]

title: Amazon employees are "tokenmaxxing" due to pressure to use AI tools
summary: Workers are using an internal AI tool to automate non-essential tasks.

title: Once again, SpaceX has set a new record for the tallest rocket ever built
summary: SpaceX cleared an important milestone Monday on the road to launching a new version of Starship.

title: Linux bitten by second severe vulnerability in as many weeks
summary: Production-version patches are coming online and should be installed pronto.

title: Audi has a new Q9 flagship coming soon: Here's its interior
summary: Audi made sure to consult American tastes for its first full-size SUV.

title: After banning foreign routers, FCC says existing ones can get updates until 2029
summary: FCC extends waiver allowing routers and drones to get patches for two more years.

title: Data center guzzled 30 million gallons of water, and nobody noticed for months
summary: Can AI save us from the AI industry’s endless thirst for water? Outlook not so good.

title: Passengers from hantavirus ship arrive in US; 3 people in biocontainment
summary: A US passenger tested "mildly positive," but WHO is calling it "inconclusive" for now.

title: Starlink shuts down its GPS-style cheat code. Researchers may unlock it anyway.

title: iOS, macOS, and iPadOS 26.5 updates arrive with encrypted RCS messaging and more
summary: Probably the last big updates we'll see before the next versions appear at WWDC.

title: Samsung made a “mockery” of Dua Lipa by putting her picture on TV boxes, lawsuit says
summary: Lipa says her picture contributed to Samsung's "enormous revenue."

title: Pirates are already playing Forza Horizon 6 days before its launch

summary: "We wanted to get to an orbital launch vehicle in a few years."

title: Sony's failed war against Internet piracy may doom other copyright lawsuits
summary: Cable firm Cox's Supreme Court win may help all tech providers, not just ISPs.

title: Do you take after your dad’s RNA?
summary: Evidence is growing that sperm carries marks of a father’s life experiences, influencing traits in offspring.

summary: These connected companions could disrupt everything from make-believe to bedtime stories. No wonder some lawmakers want them banned.

summary: It's hard to mix electronic manufacturing and flexible geometry.

summary: The plan isn't final and could change, but his ouster would be no surprise.

summary: FCC chair hasn't been able to bully ABC and owner Disney into submission.
